mechanism: Ferulic acid and related constituents show vasomodulatory, antiplatelet, and anti-inflammatory activity in preclinical and limited clinical studies.
safetyNotes: Can cause photosensitivity and GI discomfort; avoid during pregnancy due to uterine-stimulating concerns. Use caution in anticoagulated patients.

interactions: May increase bleeding risk with warfarin or antiplatelet drugs.
Possible additive effects with antihypertensive or antispasmodic therapies.
Potential estrogenic activity warrants caution with hormone-sensitive conditions.
preparation: Roots sliced, dried, decocted or tinctured; often used in TCM formulas
